views.name: MoodJournalCheckInView
views.purpose: Primary check-in screen. Shows a large animated emoji face that morphs based on the mood slider value (frown at 1, flat at 5, beaming at 10) with a background color gradient that shifts from deep red through amber to vibrant green. Below the face: a custom gradient slider (red→yellow→green) for 1-10 mood rating with haptic feedback on value changes. Then a horizontal scrollable row of emotion tag chips (pill-shaped buttons with SF Symbol + label, highlighted with hubPrimaryLight when selected). Energy level displayed as a 5-dot battery-style indicator where users tap dots to fill. Quick-tap activity bubbles in a flowing layout (exercise, work, social, family, sleep, nature, reading, music, cooking, travel) that toggle selection with a gentle scale animation. Collapsible text area for optional freeform notes. Large 'Save Check-In' button at bottom using HubButton style. If already checked in today, shows the existing entry in a review card with an 'Update' option instead of fresh entry.

views.name: MoodJournalCalendarView
views.purpose: Month calendar grid inspired by 'Year in Pixels' where each day cell is a rounded square color-coded by mood rating using a continuous gradient (deep red=1, orange=3, amber=5, lime=7, green=10, gray with dashed border=no entry). Swipe or arrow buttons for month-to-month navigation. Tapping a day cell expands it into a slide-up detail card showing that day's mood face, emotion chip, energy dots, activity tags, and note in a compact HubCard. Below the calendar grid: three summary stat cards in a horizontal row showing the month's average mood (with trend arrow vs previous month), total check-ins count, and most frequent emotion with its icon. The calendar provides an at-a-glance emotional landscape that makes weekly and monthly mood patterns immediately visible through color clustering.

views.name: MoodJournalTrendsView
views.purpose: Insights and analytics screen. Top: streak badge showing current consecutive check-in days with a flame icon, plus longest streak record. Below: a smooth line chart plotting mood ratings over the last 30 days with a dashed trend line overlay and colored zones (green band for 8-10, yellow 5-7, red 1-4). Weekly comparison card showing this week's average vs last week with a prominent percentage change arrow (green up or red down). Mood distribution horizontal bar chart breaking entries into four ranges: Great (8-10), Good (6-7), Okay (4-5), Low (1-3) with proportional colored bars and percentages. Top emotions ranked list showing the 5 most logged emotions with their icons and frequency counts. Insight cards in a vertical stack with contextual observations like 'Your mood averages 7.4 on days you exercise vs 4.8 without' and 'Weekends score 1.5 points higher than weekdays'. Energy-mood correlation indicator showing whether high energy days correlate with better mood.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: currentStreak
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Consecutive calendar days with at least one mood entry counting backwards from today. Resets to 0 if yesterday has no entry.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: longestStreak
viewModelComputedProperties.description: All-time longest consecutive daily check-in streak ever achieved.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: todayEntry
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Today's mood entry if it exists, nil if user has not checked in yet today.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weeklyAverage
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Mean mood rating for the current Monday-through-Sunday week. Returns 0 if no entries this week.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: previousWeekAverage
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Mean mood rating for the previous Monday-through-Sunday week. Used for trend comparison.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weeklyTrend
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Percentage change from previous week average to current week: ((current - previous) / previous) * 100. Positive means improving.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: monthEntries
viewModelComputedProperties.description: All entries for the currently displayed month in the calendar view, sorted by date ascending.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: last30DaysEntries
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Entries from the last 30 calendar days for the trend line chart, sorted chronologically.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: moodDistribution
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Categorizes all entries into four buckets: Great (8-10), Good (6-7), Okay (4-5), Low (1-3). Returns label, count, and percentage of total.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: topEmotions
viewModelComputedProperties.description: All logged emotions ranked by frequency descending, limited to top 5 for display.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: moodFaceSymbol
viewModelComputedProperties.description: SF Symbol name mapped from current slider value: sparkles (9-10), sun.max.fill (7-8), circle.fill (5-6), cloud.fill (3-4), cloud.rain.fill (1-2).

viewModelComputedProperties.name: moodColor
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Linearly interpolated color from the mood rating: red (1) → orange (3) → yellow (5) → lime (7) → green (10).

viewModelComputedProperties.name: totalEntries
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Total count of all mood entries ever recorded.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: averageMoodAllTime
viewModelComputedProperties.description: All-time mean mood rating across every entry.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: activityMoodCorrelations
viewModelComputedProperties.description: For each unique activity tag, computes the average mood rating on days that activity was logged. Sorted by average mood descending.

dailyGoal.description: Complete at least one mood check-in per calendar day. Goal is binary — logged or not.

domainLogic.streakDefinition: A day is 'active' if it contains at least one MoodJournalEntry. The streak counts consecutive calendar days backwards from today. If today has no entry yet, the streak still holds until end of day. Missing a single day resets the streak to 0. Milestones at 7, 14, 30, 60, 90, 180, and 365 days trigger celebration badges.
domainLogic.trendAnalysis: Primary metric: 7-day rolling average mood rating compared to the previous 7-day window, expressed as percentage change. Secondary: weekday average (Mon-Fri) vs weekend average (Sat-Sun) to detect work-life mood differential. Tertiary: Pearson correlation coefficient between energy level and mood rating over the last 30 days. Quarterly: month-over-month average mood comparison for long-term trajectory.
insights: Weekly mood trend direction with percentage change and natural language summary (e.g., 'Your mood improved 12% this week')
Best and worst days of the week by average mood rating across all historical data
Most frequently logged emotion and whether it has shifted compared to the previous 30 days
Activity-mood correlations: average mood on days each activity was logged vs days without, highlighting activities with >1.0 point differential
Energy-mood correlation strength: whether high-energy days consistently produce better mood scores
Streak milestone celebrations with encouraging messages at 7, 14, 30, 60, 90, 365 day marks
Month-over-month average mood comparison showing trajectory over the last 3-6 months
Weekend vs weekday mood gap analysis identifying work-life balance signals
